evals.prompt: I'm spawning goroutines inside a for loop to process orders in parallel. How do I make sure none of them leak?
evals.expected_output: Use errgroup or sync.WaitGroup so every goroutine has a known exit path. Pass context.Context for cancellation. Call wg.Wait() or g.Wait() to ensure all goroutines complete.

evals.prompt: Multiple goroutines need to update a shared counter. What's the idiomatic Go approach?
evals.expected_output: Use sync/atomic for simple counters or a sync.Mutex to protect shared state. Do not mutate global variables without synchronization. Prefer channels to communicate results back rather than shared mutation.

evals.prompt: How do I run go test with the race detector?
evals.expected_output: Run `go test -race ./...` to enable the race detector. Always include -race in CI to catch data races.

should_not_trigger: How do I connect to PostgreSQL in Go?
Write a REST handler in Go
Set up golangci-lint for my project
